items.how_used: 使用线性回归建立暴力犯罪率与失业率、毕业率之间的定量关系模型，通过SPSS软件进行回归分析，得到回归方程。

formulas.latex_code: V = a + b_1 \cdot U + b_2 \cdot G

variables.V: 暴力犯罪率（每10万人暴力犯罪数）

items.source_excerpt: We determined that the most effective method for deriving our model would be to employ a linear regression analysis... The regression assigns coefficient values to all of the qualifying independent variables, and puts them on one side of the equation along with a constant.

items.how_used: 计算所有变量对之间的Pearson相关系数，用于筛选与因变量相关性强的自变量，发现毕业率与袭击数相关系数为-0.742，失业率与谋杀数相关系数为0.524。
items.source_excerpt: We also measured the relationships between individual variables by using the same software to calculate the correlations (specifically Pearson correlations) between every possible pair of variables.

items.how_used: 将暴力犯罪数除以城市人口并乘以100,000，得到每10万人的暴力犯罪率，以消除人口规模差异的影响。

formulas.latex_code: \text{Rate of Incidents of Violence} = \frac{\text{Incidents of Violence}}{\text{City Population}} \times 100,000

items.source_excerpt: In order to compensate for population changes, we set up a ratio for the number of incidents of violence per 100,000 persons.

items.how_used: 使用SPSS软件进行线性回归分析和相关性计算，以提高计算效率并获得模型有效性度量。
items.source_excerpt: Because performing a regression with a large number of variables can become exceedingly complex, we used the statistics analysis program “Statistics Package for the Social Sciences” (SPSS) to analyze our numbers.

items.how_used: 使用多种统计指标（如R²、相对误差、RMSE）评估模型的准确性和有效性，但论文中未给出具体数值。
items.source_excerpt: Our model’s usefulness was substantiated by various statistical measurements that are used to measure...

items.how_used: 列出8条模型假设，包括城市无现有项目、城市可比性、其他变量无关、数据代表城市等，为模型建立提供前提。
items.source_excerpt:
ASSUMPTIONS
1. The city does not have any of our programs already in place...
2. As a whole, our city is comparable to that of a city of the same size...
3. Any factors other than the given variables are inconsequential...

items.how_used: 论文中未直接使用可视化，但通过变量定义和相关性分析间接体现了数据可视化原则，如选择关键变量进行展示。
items.source_excerpt: We also measured the relationships between individual variables by using the same software to calculate the correlations... This gave us an excellent basis for comparing the relative strengths of the variables in terms of their correlation with the dependent variable.
